user_prompt: edit `pump_mic2bt` inside `lib\AudioManager\AudioManager.cpp`, if the mic is the spm1423 mic, then the stream is mono, if the mic is the wm8960 mic, the stream is stereo. Add a `queueStereo` function to the `AudioFifo` class. The data inside this AudioFifo is always 16 kHz mono
agent_reply: - `queueStereo(const int16_t* interleavedSamples, size_t frameCount, uint32_t sampleRateHz = 16000)`
It accepts interleaved stereo frames, mixes each frame to mono with `(L + R) / 2`, and stores the FIFO’s internal stream as 16 kHz mono. It also supports the existing 8 kHz upsample path if needed.
- Sends both `mic2bt` and `mic2file` through the same normalization path

user_prompt: class `AudioFifo`, function `availableToRead`, if the state is `State::Filling` and the watermark criteria is not met, do not return `usedSamples_`, return 0 instead to fool the caller.
agent_reply: Updated `AudioFifo::availableToRead()` so it now returns `0` while `state_ == State::Filling` and the watermark hasn’t been met. It calls `updateWatermarkLocked()` first, so once the watermark criteria is satisfied, it flips to draining and returns `usedSamples_`.

Updated `AudioFifo::availableToRead()` so it now returns `0` while `state_ == State::Filling` and the watermark hasn’t been met. It calls `updateWatermarkLocked()` first, so once the watermark criteria is satisfied, it flips to draining and returns `usedSamples_`.

user_prompt: inside `lib\AudioFifo\AudioFifo.h`, why does both `getFillPercentagle` and `getFillPercentage` exist?
agent_reply: Because `getFillPercentagle()` is almost certainly a typo that became part of the API, and `getFillPercentage()` was later added as the correctly spelled wrapper.
In [AudioFifo.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h), the typo version does the real work:
and the correctly spelled one just forwards to it:

So functionally they are identical. The likely reason both exist is backward compatibility: some code may have used the misspelled name, then the correctly spelled name was added without breaking existing callers.
If you want to clean it up, the tidy path is: search for `getFillPercentagle`, replace any callers with `getFillPercentage`, then remove the misspelled method.

user_prompt: review the changes I made to `AudioFifo::getFillPercentage`
agent_reply: - [AudioFifo.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:282): the “full” test is slightly too loose: `usedSamples_ >= (capacitySamples_ - 1)` can report `100` when the FIFO is one sample short of full. In normal current capacities, the `percentage >= 100` branch will only be reached when `usedSamples_ == capacitySamples_`, so it probably won’t bite today. But it contradicts the comment and makes the edge behavior fragile. I’d make the condition exact:

The main idea looks good: for the recorder’s `kPumpTargetFillPercentage = 0`, returning `1` for any non-empty FIFO means the pump loop won’t falsely stop while a tiny unread tail remains. That matches your intent nicely.
No major correctness issues beyond the off-by-one-ish full check and readability of the nested ternary.

- [AudioFifo.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:282): the “full” test is slightly too loose: `usedSamples_ >= (capacitySamples_ - 1)` can report `100` when the FIFO is one sample short of full. In normal current capacities, the `percentage >= 100` branch will only be reached when `usedSamples_ == capacitySamples_`, so it probably won’t bite today. But it contradicts the comment and makes the edge behavior fragile. I’d make the condition exact:

No major correctness issues beyond the off-by-one-ish full check and readability of the nested ternary.

commit_message: fifo reports percentage better, no zeros or 100 unless actually empty or full

user_prompt: in `lib\AudioFifo\AudioFifo.h`, add something similar to `muted` but call it `choked`, it simply won't let queue happen, but return success.
there are operating modes that don't use Bluetooth, like a local memo recording, and I want this as a mechanism to not need having to drain the BT FIFO
when this is implemented, let the `test_micrec` simply set the choke flag instead of draining the FIFO
agent_reply: In [AudioFifo.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:74), `choked` now behaves like a sink:
- `availableToWrite()` reports full capacity while choked, so producers like `pump_mic2bt()` keep running.
In [test_micrec.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_micrec.cpp:165), the test now chokes `micToBluetoothFifo()` instead of draining it, and unchokes it on stop/failure.
Verification: `pio run` passed successfully. There is still the existing packed-member warning in `AudioFileRecorder.cpp`, unrelated to this change.

commit_message: audiofifo can be choked, so local recordings don't stuff up the bt fifo
